description: Fix inaccurate reference documentation: correct secret storage location references (auth.json not config.json), add missing CLI command (opentabs update), fix incomplete doctor checks list, and add missing --show-secret flag to config show docs.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip && bun run format:check

userStories.title: Fix secret storage location in MCP server reference
userStories.description: As a user reading the MCP server docs, I want accurate information about where the authentication secret is stored so that I can correctly configure my setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Line 86 of mcp-server.mdx no longer says the token comes from ~/.opentabs/config.json — it says ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json
Line 314 of mcp-server.mdx no longer says 'the same secret from ~/.opentabs/config.json' — it references auth.json as the source of truth
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx. Two locations to fix:
1. Line 86 in the /mcp table: change 'the 64-char hex token from `~/.opentabs/config.json`' to reference `~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json`.
2. Line 314 in the Authentication section: change 'the same `secret` from `~/.opentabs/config.json`' to reference auth.json.
The secret is stored exclusively in auth.json (see platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts loadSecret() function). The CLI's `config show --json` merges the secret from auth.json into the output for convenience, but auth.json is the source of truth.

userStories.title: Fix secret field in configuration reference
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the configuration docs, I want to know the correct location and schema of the authentication secret so that I don't look for it in the wrong file.
acceptanceCriteria: The config fields table no longer lists `secret` as a field of config.json — instead there is a separate section explaining that the secret lives in auth.json
The example config JSON no longer includes a 'secret' field
The 'secret' subsection under 'Field details' explains that the secret is in ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json, not config.json
The `opentabs config show --json | jq -r .secret` command is still documented (it works because the CLI merges the secret from auth.json) but with a note clarifying the actual storage location
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx.

The actual OpentabsConfig type in platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts has these fields: localPlugins, tools, browserToolPolicy, permissions, skipConfirmation. No `secret` field.

The secret is stored in ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json as { "secret": "<hex>" }. The config.json file does NOT contain the secret.

Changes needed:
1. Remove `secret` row from the config fields table (around line 58-63)
2. Remove `"secret": "a1b2c3d4e5f6..."` from the example config JSON (line 93)
3. Rewrite the '### secret' section (around line 127) to explain that the secret lives in auth.json, not config.json. The CLI's `config show --json` merges it from auth.json for convenience, which is why `jq -r .secret` works.
4. Add a brief mention in the 'Auth file' or similar section about ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json containing { "secret": "<hex>" }.

Keep the existing documentation about what the secret is used for (MCP endpoint auth, WebSocket auth) — just correct WHERE it's stored.

userStories.title: Fix CLI reference: add missing update command, --show-secret flag, and complete doctor checks
userStories.description: As a user reading the CLI reference, I want complete and accurate documentation of all commands, flags, and diagnostic checks.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'opentabs update' section exists in the CLI docs with description, behavior, and example usage
The 'opentabs config show' options table includes the --show-secret flag

The 'opentabs doctor' checks list includes all 11 checks: runtime, browser, config file, auth secret, MCP server health, extension connected, extension installed, extension version, MCP client config, local plugins, npm plugins
The rotate-secret section does NOT claim it writes to config.json — it correctly states it writes to auth.json only
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx.

Four changes needed:

1. ADD 'opentabs update' section (after 'opentabs config' section, before end of file). The command: checks current vs latest version via npm view, warns if server is running, runs npm install -g @opentabs-dev/cli@<version>, advises user to restart MCP server. No flags. See platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts for details.

2. ADD --show-secret flag to the 'opentabs config show' options table. It displays the full authentication secret instead of masking it. See platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts line 575.

3. UPDATE the 'opentabs doctor' checks list. Current docs list 9 checks but source has 11. The missing checks are:
   - 'Browser' (check #2): Verifies a Chromium-based browser is installed (Chrome, Chromium, Edge, Brave). Checks /Applications/ on macOS, which on Linux, Program Files on Windows.
   - 'Auth secret' (check #4): Verifies ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json contains a valid secret.
   See platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts for the full list.

4. FIX the 'rotate-secret' section (around line 639). Currently says 'Generate a new 256-bit random secret and write it to both config.json and auth.json.' It should say it writes to auth.json only (the secret is NOT in config.json). See platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts for the rotate-secret implementation.
